# ============================================================
# ASHA 2024 SLP Schools — State Analysis 
# Author: Yuchen Ding
# Date: 2026-04-10
# ============================================================
```{r}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Loading Library
# ------------------------------------------------------------
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(scales)
library(broom)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Load Data
# ------------------------------------------------------------
resp <- readxl::read_excel("/Users/asuka/Downloads/OneDrive_1_3-24-2026/2024 SLPs Schools Respondent File for Capstone.xlsx")
full <- readxl::read_excel("/Users/asuka/Downloads/OneDrive_1_3-24-2026/2024 SLPs Schools Full Sample for Capstone.xlsx")

cat("Respondent file n =", nrow(resp), "\n")
cat("Full sample file n =", nrow(full), "\n\n")
cat("Respondent columns:\n"); print(names(resp))
cat("\nFull sample columns:\n"); print(names(full))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Transmute / Clean
# ------------------------------------------------------------
resp2 <- resp %>%
  transmute(
    new_id         = as.integer(NewID),
    mode           = as.integer(mode),
    emp_resp_raw   = rq2stat,
    facility_resp_raw = Q11_facility,
    state_resp     = toupper(as.character(Q27_state))
  )

full2 <- full %>%
  transmute(
    new_id           = as.integer(NewID),
    mode             = as.integer(Mode),
    facility_frame_raw = PrimaryFacility,
    employment_frame = as.character(EmploymentStatusValue),
    state_frame      = toupper(as.character(State)),
    age              = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(Age)),
    response_code    = as.character(`Repsonse Code`)
  )


# Mode labels
resp2 <- resp2 %>%
  mutate(
    mode_label = case_when(
      mode == 1 ~ "Postal mail",
      mode == 2 ~ "SurveyMonkey",
      TRUE      ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

full2 <- full2 %>%
  mutate(
    mode_label = case_when(
      mode == 1 ~ "Postal mail",
      mode == 2 ~ "SurveyMonkey",
      TRUE      ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

# Respondent employment
resp2 <- resp2 %>%
  mutate(
    emp_resp = case_when(
      emp_resp_raw == 1 ~ "Employed full time",
      emp_resp_raw == 2 ~ "Employed part time",
      TRUE              ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

# Frame facility recode
full2 <- full2 %>%
  mutate(
    facility_frame = case_when(
      facility_frame_raw %in% c(101, 102, 107) ~ "Special school",
      facility_frame_raw == 103               ~ "Pre-elementary",
      facility_frame_raw == 104               ~ "Elementary",
      facility_frame_raw == 105               ~ "Secondary",
      facility_frame_raw == 106               ~ "Combination",
      facility_frame_raw == 108               ~ "Admin office",
      facility_frame_raw == 199               ~ "Other",
      TRUE                                    ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

# Respondent facility recode
resp2 <- resp2 %>%
  mutate(
    facility_resp = case_when(
      facility_resp_raw == 1 ~ "Special school",
      facility_resp_raw == 2 ~ "Pre-elementary",
      facility_resp_raw == 3 ~ "Elementary",
      facility_resp_raw == 4 ~ "Secondary",
      facility_resp_raw == 5 ~ "Home",
      facility_resp_raw == 6 ~ "Admin office",
      facility_resp_raw == 7 ~ "Telepractice",
      facility_resp_raw == 8 ~ "Combination",
      facility_resp_raw == 9 ~ "Other",
      TRUE                   ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

# Age groups
full2 <- full2 %>%
  mutate(
    age_group = case_when(
      is.na(age)            ~ NA_character_,
      age < 35              ~ "<35",
      age >= 35 & age < 45 ~ "35-44",
      age >= 45 & age < 55 ~ "45-54",
      age >= 55 & age < 65 ~ "55-64",
      age >= 65             ~ "65+",
      TRUE                  ~ NA_character_
    )
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Census Region Recode (US Census Bureau 4-region standard)
# ------------------------------------------------------------
northeast_states <- c(
  "CT", "ME", "MA", "NH", "RI", "VT",   # New England
  "NJ", "NY", "PA"                        # Middle Atlantic
)

midwest_states <- c(
  "IL", "IN", "MI", "OH", "WI",          # East North Central
  "IA", "KS", "MN", "MO", "NE", "ND", "SD"  # West North Central
)

south_states <- c(
  "DE", "FL", "GA", "MD", "NC", "SC", "VA", "DC", "WV",  # South Atlantic
  "AL", "KY", "MS", "TN",                                  # East South Central
  "AR", "LA", "OK", "TX"                                   # West South Central
)

west_states <- c(
  "AZ", "CO", "ID", "MT", "NV", "NM", "UT", "WY",  # Mountain
  "AK", "CA", "HI", "OR", "WA"                       # Pacific
)

region_recode <- function(state_var) {
  case_when(
    state_var %in% northeast_states ~ "Northeast",
    state_var %in% midwest_states   ~ "Midwest",
    state_var %in% south_states     ~ "South",
    state_var %in% west_states      ~ "West",
    TRUE                            ~ NA_character_
  )
}

full2 <- full2 %>%
  mutate(census_region = region_recode(state_frame))

resp2 <- resp2 %>%
  mutate(census_region = region_recode(state_resp))

cat("\nCensus region distribution (full sample):\n")
print(table(full2$census_region, useNA = "ifany"))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Response and Eligibility Coding
# ------------------------------------------------------------
postal_ineligible <- c(
  "2 - Bad Address",
  "4 - Ineligible: Deceased",
  "5 - Ineligible: Retired",
  "6 - Ineligible: Wrong Facility, etc.",
  "7 - Ineligible: Not working in the professions",
  "8 - Ineligible: Other Reason"
)

postal_refusal <- c(
  "9 - Refusal: Upset with ASHA",
  "10 - Refusal: No reason given",
  "11 - Refusal: other"
)

full3 <- full2 %>%
  mutate(
    responded_from_frame = case_when(
      mode == 1 & response_code == "1"   ~ 1L,
      mode == 2 & response_code == "Yes" ~ 1L,
      TRUE                               ~ 0L
    ),
    responded_from_respfile = if_else(new_id %in% resp2$new_id, 1L, 0L),
    responded = if_else(
      responded_from_frame == 1L | responded_from_respfile == 1L,
      1L, 0L
    ),
    eligible = case_when(
      mode == 1 & response_code %in% postal_ineligible         ~ 0L,
      mode == 2 & response_code %in% c("Bounced", "Opted out") ~ 0L,
      TRUE                                                      ~ 1L
    ),
    disposition = case_when(
      responded == 1L                                          ~ "Complete",
      mode == 1 & response_code %in% postal_ineligible         ~ "Ineligible",
      mode == 2 & response_code %in% c("Bounced", "Opted out") ~ "Ineligible",
      mode == 1 & response_code %in% postal_refusal            ~ "Refusal",
      mode == 1 & response_code == "3 - Resent"                ~ "Resent",
      is.na(response_code)                                     ~ "Blank / no response",
      TRUE                                                     ~ "Other nonresponse"
    )
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 7. QA Check
# ------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n================ QA check =====================\n")
qa_check <- full3 %>%
  summarize(
    n_total             = n(),
    frame_responders    = sum(responded_from_frame,    na.rm = TRUE),
    respfile_responders = sum(responded_from_respfile, na.rm = TRUE),
    both          = sum(responded_from_frame == 1 & responded_from_respfile == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    frame_only    = sum(responded_from_frame == 1 & responded_from_respfile == 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    respfile_only = sum(responded_from_frame == 0 & responded_from_respfile == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
print(qa_check)

mismatch_ids <- full3 %>%
  filter(responded_from_frame != responded_from_respfile) %>%
  select(new_id, mode_label, response_code,
         responded_from_frame, responded_from_respfile)
cat("\nNumber of mismatched IDs =", nrow(mismatch_ids), "\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 8. Disposition Counts
# ------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n================ Overall disposition ====================\n")
disp_overall <- full3 %>% count(disposition, sort = TRUE)
print(disp_overall, n = Inf)

cat("\n================ Disposition by mode ====================\n")
disp_mode <- full3 %>%
  count(mode_label, disposition) %>%
  group_by(mode_label) %>%
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup()
print(disp_mode, n = Inf)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 9. Helper Function: response_rate_fun
# ------------------------------------------------------------
response_rate_fun <- function(df, group_var = NULL) {
  if (is.null(group_var)) {
    df %>%
      summarize(
        sample_n      = n(),
        eligible_n    = sum(eligible,  na.rm = TRUE),
        complete_n    = sum(responded, na.rm = TRUE),
        response_rate = complete_n / eligible_n
      )
  } else {
    df %>%
      group_by(.data[[group_var]]) %>%
      summarize(
        sample_n      = n(),
        eligible_n    = sum(eligible,  na.rm = TRUE),
        complete_n    = sum(responded, na.rm = TRUE),
        response_rate = complete_n / eligible_n,
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
      arrange(desc(response_rate))
  }
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 10. Overall Response Rate
# ------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n================ Overall response rate ==================\n")
overall_rr <- response_rate_fun(full3)
print(overall_rr)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 11. Response Rate by Mode
# ------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n================ Response rate by mode ==================\n")
rr_mode <- response_rate_fun(full3, "mode_label")
print(rr_mode)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 12. Response Rate by Employment
# ------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n================ Response rate by frame employment ======\n")
rr_emp <- response_rate_fun(full3, "employment_frame")
print(rr_emp)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 13. Response Rate by Age Group
# ------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n================ Response rate by age group =============\n")
rr_age <- full3 %>%
  filter(!is.na(age_group)) %>%
  group_by(age_group) %>%
  summarize(
    sample_n      = n(),
    eligible_n    = sum(eligible,  na.rm = TRUE),
    complete_n    = sum(responded, na.rm = TRUE),
    response_rate = complete_n / eligible_n,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(response_rate))
print(rr_age)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 14. Response Rate by Facility
# ------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n================ Response rate by facility ==============\n")
rr_facility <- full3 %>%
  filter(!is.na(facility_frame)) %>%
  group_by(facility_frame) %>%
  summarize(
    sample_n      = n(),
    eligible_n    = sum(eligible,  na.rm = TRUE),
    complete_n    = sum(responded, na.rm = TRUE),
    response_rate = complete_n / eligible_n,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(response_rate))
print(rr_facility, n = Inf)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 15. Response Rate by State
# ------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n================ Response rate by state (eligible_n >= 30) =====\n")
rr_state <- full3 %>%
  filter(!is.na(state_frame)) %>%
  group_by(state_frame) %>%
  summarize(
    sample_n      = n(),
    eligible_n    = sum(eligible,  na.rm = TRUE),
    complete_n    = sum(responded, na.rm = TRUE),
    response_rate = complete_n / eligible_n,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(eligible_n >= 30) %>%
  arrange(desc(response_rate))
print(rr_state, n = Inf)

# Top 15 states bar chart
rr_state_top15 <- rr_state %>% slice_max(order_by = response_rate, n = 15)

ggplot(rr_state_top15,
       aes(x = reorder(state_frame, response_rate), y = response_rate)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#4472C4") +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Top 15 states by eligibility-adjusted response rate",
    x = "State", y = "Response rate"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 16. Response Rate by Census Region
# ------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n================ Response rate by Census region =========\n")
rr_region <- response_rate_fun(full3 %>% filter(!is.na(census_region)), "census_region")
print(rr_region)

# Response rate by Census region AND mode
cat("\n================ Response rate by region × mode =========\n")
rr_region_mode <- full3 %>%
  filter(!is.na(census_region), !is.na(mode_label)) %>%
  group_by(census_region, mode_label) %>%
  summarize(
    sample_n      = n(),
    eligible_n    = sum(eligible,  na.rm = TRUE),
    complete_n    = sum(responded, na.rm = TRUE),
    response_rate = complete_n / eligible_n,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = mode_label,
    values_from = c(eligible_n, complete_n, response_rate)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    delta_pp = (`response_rate_Postal mail` - `response_rate_SurveyMonkey`) * 100
  )
print(rr_region_mode)

# Plot: response rate by region × mode
rr_region_mode_long <- full3 %>%
  filter(!is.na(census_region), !is.na(mode_label)) %>%
  group_by(census_region, mode_label) %>%
  summarize(
    eligible_n    = sum(eligible,  na.rm = TRUE),
    complete_n    = sum(responded, na.rm = TRUE),
    response_rate = complete_n / eligible_n,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot(rr_region_mode_long,
       aes(x = census_region, y = response_rate, fill = mode_label)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Postal mail" = "#4472C4", "SurveyMonkey" = "#ED7D31")) +
  labs(
    title = "Response rate by Census region and mode",
    x = "Census region", y = "Response rate", fill = "Mode"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 17. Chi-Square: Response by State
# ------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n================ Chi-square: response by state ==========\n")
tab_state   <- table(full3$responded, full3$state_frame)
chisq_state <- chisq.test(tab_state)
print(chisq_state)
cat(sprintf(
  "\nSummary: chi-square = %.2f, df = %d, p-value %s\n",
  chisq_state$statistic,
  chisq_state$parameter,
  ifelse(chisq_state$p.value < 0.001, "< 0.001", round(chisq_state$p.value, 3))
))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 18. Chi-Square: Response by Census Region
# ------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n================ Chi-square: response by Census region ==\n")
tab_region   <- table(full3$responded, full3$census_region)
chisq_region <- chisq.test(tab_region)
print(chisq_region)
cat(sprintf(
  "\nSummary: chi-square = %.2f, df = %d, p-value %s\n",
  chisq_region$statistic,
  chisq_region$parameter,
  ifelse(chisq_region$p.value < 0.001, "< 0.001", round(chisq_region$p.value, 3))
))

# Chi-square: response by region, within each mode
cat("\n--- Chi-square by region, Postal mail only ---\n")
tab_region_mail <- table(
  full3$responded[full3$mode == 1],
  full3$census_region[full3$mode == 1]
)
print(chisq.test(tab_region_mail))

cat("\n--- Chi-square by region, SurveyMonkey only ---\n")
tab_region_web <- table(
  full3$responded[full3$mode == 2],
  full3$census_region[full3$mode == 2]
)
print(chisq.test(tab_region_web))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 19. LRT: Mode × Region Interaction
# ------------------------------------------------------------
cat("\n================ LRT: mode × region interaction =========\n")

lrt_data <- full3 %>%
  filter(!is.na(census_region), !is.na(mode_label), eligible == 1)

# Main effects only
glm_main <- glm(
  responded ~ mode_label + census_region,
  data   = lrt_data,
  family = binomial
)

# With interaction
glm_int <- glm(
  responded ~ mode_label * census_region,
  data   = lrt_data,
  family = binomial
)

lrt_result <- anova(glm_main, glm_int, test = "LRT")
print(lrt_result)

delta_dev <- lrt_result$Deviance[2]
df_lrt    <- lrt_result$Df[2]
p_lrt     <- lrt_result$`Pr(>Chi)`[2]

cat(sprintf(
  "\nLRT summary: ΔDeviance = %.2f, df = %d, p = %.3f\n",
  delta_dev, df_lrt, p_lrt
))
cat(if_else(
  p_lrt >= 0.05,
  "→ Adding interaction does NOT improve model fit: mode effect is uniform across Census regions.\n",
  "→ Significant interaction: mode effect DIFFERS by Census region.\n"
))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 20. Composition Comparison Helper
# ------------------------------------------------------------
composition_compare <- function(data, varname) {
  eligible_dist <- data %>%
    filter(eligible == 1, !is.na(.data[[varname]])) %>%
    count(.data[[varname]], name = "eligible_n") %>%
    mutate(eligible_pct = eligible_n / sum(eligible_n))

  respondent_dist <- data %>%
    filter(eligible == 1, responded == 1, !is.na(.data[[varname]])) %>%
    count(.data[[varname]], name = "respondent_n") %>%
    mutate(respondent_pct = respondent_n / sum(respondent_n))

  full_join(eligible_dist, respondent_dist,
            by = setNames(varname, varname)) %>%
    mutate(
      eligible_n     = replace_na(eligible_n,    0),
      respondent_n   = replace_na(respondent_n,  0),
      eligible_pct   = replace_na(eligible_pct,  0),
      respondent_pct = replace_na(respondent_pct, 0),
      diff_pct_points = 100 * (respondent_pct - eligible_pct)
    ) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs(diff_pct_points)))
}


cat("\n================ Composition: state =====================\n")
comp_state <- composition_compare(full3 %>% filter(!is.na(state_frame)), "state_frame")
cat("\nTop 20 states by absolute composition deviation:\n")
print(comp_state %>% arrange(desc(abs(diff_pct_points))), n = 20)

# Plot top 15 state deviations
comp_state_plot <- comp_state %>%
  slice_max(order_by = abs(diff_pct_points), n = 15) %>%
  mutate(direction = if_else(diff_pct_points > 0, "Over-represented", "Under-represented"))

ggplot(comp_state_plot,
       aes(x = reorder(state_frame, diff_pct_points),
           y = diff_pct_points, fill = direction)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Over-represented"  = "#4472C4",
                               "Under-represented" = "#ED7D31")) +
  labs(
    title = "State composition: respondents vs. eligible sample (top 15 deviations)",
    x = "State",
    y = "Difference (respondent % \u2212 eligible %)",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

cat("\n================ Composition: Census region =============\n")
comp_region <- composition_compare(full3 %>% filter(!is.na(census_region)), "census_region")
print(comp_region)

# Plot region composition deviations
comp_region_plot <- comp_region %>%
  mutate(direction = if_else(diff_pct_points > 0, "Over-represented", "Under-represented"))

ggplot(comp_region_plot,
       aes(x = reorder(census_region, diff_pct_points),
           y = diff_pct_points, fill = direction)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Over-represented"  = "#4472C4",
                               "Under-represented" = "#ED7D31")) +
  labs(
    title = "Census region composition: respondents vs. eligible sample",
    x = "Census region",
    y = "Difference (respondent % \u2212 eligible %)",
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```
